subject: Statistics
title: The Confidence Interval Debate
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Confidence Interval Debate.

hook.text: A 95% confidence interval does NOT mean 95% probability the true value is inside. Surprised?

buildup.text: Frequentist intervals describe a procedure: 95% of intervals from repeated samples would contain the truth.

discovery.text: Bayesian credible intervals say directly: there's a 95% probability the parameter is in this range.

twist.text: Bayesians say confidence intervals answer the wrong question. The debate reveals deep philosophical divides.

climax.text: With enough data, both intervals give nearly identical numbers. The fight is about meaning, not math.

punchline.text: Same numbers, different meanings. One is more intuitive.

quiz.question: What can a Bayesian credible interval claim that a confidence interval cannot?
options: A direct probability statement that the parameter is in the interval
That the interval is always narrower
